response.justification: Candidate has extensive software development experience but fundamentally misses all three critical requirements for this role. Only ~6 months of C++ work mentioned (vs. mandatory 5-10 years), no capital markets/fixed income domain expertise, and career trajectory is web/DevOps-focused with no Windows platform development. While communication skills are strong and some technologies overlap (SQL, Java, CI/CD), the gaps in core C++ proficiency and financial domain knowledge make this a poor fit for a mid-senior capital markets role.
strengths: Excellent communication and teaching demonstrated through multiple workshops and technical talks
20+ years of software development experience with ability to learn diverse technologies and domains
Relevant supplementary skills: SQL (basic), Java experience, CI/CD pipeline knowledge, and DevOps infrastructure background
concerns: Critical gap: Only ~6 months of C++ work at National Instruments vs. mandatory 5-10 years requirement
Zero documented experience with capital markets, fixed income, derivatives, or investment banking systems
Career trajectory is web/startup/mobile/DevOps focused; no evidence of Windows platform development or financial systems expertise
